title: Declarative routes and links

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Declarative routes and links; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Router Documentation; stable declarative mode accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Declarative routes and links
sources.claim: Client routing maps URLs to views while preserving addressability, history, refresh behavior, and server fallback configuration.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Client routing maps URLs to views while preserving addressability, history, refresh behavior, and server fallback configuration.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Navigating to /courses/c renders the course view and refreshing that URL is served by configured hosting. Your goal is to implement nested routes and verify direct, link and back-forward navigation. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Remote data and asynchronous UI; javascript browser url; Children and composition. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Client routing maps URLs to views while preserving addressability, history, refresh behavior, and server fallback configuration. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Nested routes, base paths, loaders, not-found states, and direct deployment requests require router-specific setup. This draft therefore rejects: A client router makes every deep URL work on the server automatically.

includes: Map paths to page-level components and navigate with real links that preserve browser behavior.
Implement nested routes and verify direct, link and back-forward navigation.
excludes: Nested routes, base paths, loaders, not-found states, and direct deployment requests require router-specific setup.

outcomes: Implement nested routes and verify direct, link and back-forward navigation.

examples.title: Declarative routes and links: concrete case

walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: Navigating to /courses/c renders the course view and refreshing that URL is served by configured hosting.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Configure hosting for direct route requests and test navigation, refresh, back, forward, and not-found behavior.
